title: Selecting the Right GAN for a Task
concept: Choose cGAN for labeled synthesis, Pix2Pix for paired translation, CycleGAN for style mapping, and WGAN-GP for general training stability.

exercises.prompt: Why do we need to choose different GAN architectures for different projects select different types of GAN architectures exist?

options.text: Every GAN architecture behaves the same way regardless of the task.
Different GANs are designed for different tasks, like style transfer or stable training.
We only use GANs when we have no training data at all.

exercises.explain: Selecting the right GAN architecture depends on what kind of data you have (paired vs. unpaired) and what control you want over the output.

cards.title: The GAN Toolbox
cards.body: Not all Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) are built the same! Just like you wouldn't use a screwdriver to drive a nail, picking the wrong GAN for your project can lead to bad results or training crashes. Let's learn how to choose the perfect tool for the job.

cards.title: Selecting Your GAN
cards.body: Different GANs have unique superpowers. Conditional GANs (cGAN) let you specify what to generate using labels. Pix2Pix translates images with exact pairs (like maps to satellite views). CycleGAN translates images without matching pairs (like turning horses into zebras). WGAN-GP focuses on fixing training bugs and stability.

cards.title: The Kitchen Toolkit
cards.body: Imagine you are opening a restaurant. If you want to serve whatever food you feel like making, you use a standard WGAN-GP for basic quality. If you want to cook a specific order from a menu, you use a cGAN. If you want to translate a recipe from Italian to French, you use Pix2Pix. And if you want to turn a photo of a kitchen into a painting of a kitchen without a direct template, you use CycleGAN!

cards.title: Real-World Matchup
cards.body: If you want to build a tool that turns hand-drawn architectural sketches into realistic building photos, you have direct pairs (sketch + photo). You should choose Pix2Pix. If you just want to generate high-quality random cat faces without training crashes, choose WGAN-GP.

cards.title: The 'One-Size-Fits-All' Myth
cards.body: Many beginners think that one single GAN model can do everything perfectly. In reality, a GAN designed for style swapping (like CycleGAN) will perform poorly if you try to use it for simple labeled image creation, and vice versa. Always match the tool to the task!

cards.title: Paired vs. Unpaired Translation
cards.body: Choosing the right GAN depends on whether you have paired dataset examples or unpaired datasets.

right.text: Works on loose collections, like a set of horse photos and a separate set of zebra photos.

left.text: Requires exact matched pairs, like a road map aligned perfectly with its satellite photo.

exercises.prompt: Which GAN variant is specifically designed to solve training instability and prevent common training failures?
exercises.explain: WGAN-GP introduces a gradient penalty that stabilizes the training process, helping to prevent the generator and discriminator from falling out of balance.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A Conditional GAN (cGAN) allows you to control the category or label of the image that the generator produces.
exercises.explain: cGAN (Conditional GAN) allows you to pass a specific label (like 'dog' or 'cat') along with the noise vector to guide the generator's output.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the statement about style translation:
exercises.sentence: When you want to transfer style between two domains without paired training images, you should choose ________.
exercises.explain: CycleGAN is the perfect choice when you want to map styles between two domains (like paintings and photos) but do not have matching pairs for training.

exercises.prompt: Which GAN is designed for image-to-image translation but requires exact, matching pairs of images during training?
exercises.explain: Pix2Pix requires paired training data (like a sketch and its corresponding colored photo) to learn the direct mapping from one to the other.

exercises.prompt: You want to build an app that changes photos of summer landscapes into snowy winter landscapes. However, you do not have identical matching photo pairs of the exact same places in different seasons. Which GAN architecture should you choose?

options.text: WGAN-GP, because it stabilizes the training of normal random image generation.
CycleGAN, because it translates styles between two domains without needing paired matching photos.
Conditional GAN (cGAN), because we have specific class labels for every single tree.
Pix2Pix, because it requires exact pixel-to-pixel paired photos of the same landscape.

exercises.explain: CycleGAN is designed for unpaired image-to-image translation. Since we don't have direct side-by-side matching pairs of summer and winter photos, CycleGAN uses a cycle-consistency loss to map the styles back and forth without needing exact pairs.
